prompt:
Implement a Facility Commerce & Operations API Suite for a multi-service training and retail facility that sells merchandise, schedules courses and proctored sessions, and bills on-site EV charging. The system defines clear roles and permissions: Administrators configure catalogs, promotions, and pricing policies; Operations Managers manage course/class schedules, instructor assignments, capacity, and waitlists; Proctors and Instructors record attendance and violations; Sales Associates create orders and apply vouchers at checkout; Auditors access immutable logs and reconciliation exports. The API exposes capability groups for Authentication (local username/password login, session issuance, role/permission queries), Catalog/Search (full-text keyword search with combined filters for category, attributes, and fitment dimensions, plus sorting and “did-you-mean” term suggestions), Promotions (configure spend-and-save, percentage/amount discounts, bundles, member pricing tiers, and vouchers with time windows and applicability), Courses/Classes (course categories, difficulty, age and foundation prerequisites, scheduled class instances, capacity and waitlist operations, publish/unpublish, and version history retrieval), Charging Sessions (start/end/exception/compensation tracking tied to a customer and charger asset), and Orders/Reconciliation (order creation, pricing breakdown, finalized billing results, and exportable reconciliation states). Search responses must include matched items, applied filters, sort metadata, and optional suggested terms; report responses must include KPI datasets and a persisted export reference for offline download by internal client systems.

The backend uses Fastify to implement a single-node, Docker-runnable API, with Drizzle for type-safe persistence into a local SQLite database (no internet dependencies). Data models include Users (unique username, salted password hash, role enum, failed-login counter, last-login timestamp), Products (SKU unique, name, category, attributes JSON, fitment dimensions JSON, active flag, FTS index on name/description), Promotions (type enum, priority integer 1–100, stackability enum: exclusive/stackable, max redemptions per user default 1, validity window stored in local time, applicability selectors), Vouchers (code unique, optional customer binding, expiration), Courses and ClassInstances (capacity 1–200, waitlist size cap 50, instructor assignment, publish state, version integer with change notes), Attendance (present/absent/violation enums), ChargingSessions (status enum: started/ended/exception/compensated, metered kWh decimal(10,3), start/end timestamps), Orders (idempotency key unique for 24 hours, status draft/finalized/canceled/refunded, line items, taxes, deposits and balances), Payments (offline method enum such as cash/check/manual-card-entry, reference text, amount, timestamp), and AuditLogs (append-only, actor, action, before/after hashes). Business rules include auto-selecting the best eligible offer by highest savings, blocking voucher reuse after redemption, canceling unpaid draft orders after 30 minutes, enforcing capacity before confirming enrollment, and requiring reconciliation transitions to be linear and timestamped. Non-functional requirements include median search latency under 200 ms for 50,000 products using SQLite FTS with an in-process LRU cache (5-minute TTL), local spell correction via edit-distance against catalog terms, AES-GCM encryption for sensitive fields (payment references, personal notes), structured logs with correlation IDs, and daily CSV exports for analytics and reconciliation stored on the local filesystem.

backend stack: Node.js

database: SQLite
